meta.title: KeychainPGP — Simple, Cross-Platform OpenPGP Encryption
meta.description: Encrypt and decrypt messages directly from your clipboard. Free, open-source PGP encryption for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and the web.
hero.tagline: Simple, cross-platform OpenPGP encryption for your clipboard.
hero.subtitle: Copy. Encrypt. Paste.
hero.description: KeychainPGP brings PGP encryption to everyone. No command line, no complexity — just copy your text, encrypt with a hotkey, and paste the result.
features.title: Why KeychainPGP?
features.clipboard.title: Clipboard-First
features.clipboard.description: Encrypt and decrypt directly from your clipboard. Works with any app — email, chat, notes, anywhere you can paste.
features.crypto.title: Zero-Config Crypto
features.crypto.description: Modern Ed25519 + X25519 keys by default. No algorithm choices, no key size decisions. Secure out of the box.
features.crossplatform.title: Cross-Platform
features.crossplatform.description: Available on Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and as a web app. Your keys, everywhere.
features.opensource.title: Open Source
features.opensource.description: Fully auditable code under MIT/Apache-2.0 license. No telemetry, no tracking, no accounts required.
howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1.title: Copy
howItWorks.step1.description: Copy the text you want to encrypt from any application.
howItWorks.step2.title: Encrypt
howItWorks.step2.description: Press Ctrl+Shift+E. KeychainPGP encrypts your clipboard content instantly.
howItWorks.step3.title: Paste
howItWorks.step3.description: Paste the encrypted message wherever you need it. Only the recipient can read it.
download.title: Download KeychainPGP
download.subtitle: Free and open source. No account required.
donate.title: Support the Project
donate.subtitle: KeychainPGP is free and open source. If you find it useful, consider donating directly — no middlemen, no tracking.
footer.privacy: No telemetry. No tracking. No accounts.
footer.madeWith: Built with Rust, Svelte, and Sequoia-PGP.
mockup.encrypt.example: Meeting tomorrow at 9am. Bring the documents we discussed.
mockup.decrypt.label: Encrypted message
cta.heading: Try PGP Encryption Now
cta.description: Encrypt and decrypt messages directly in your browser. No installation required.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Is it safe to encrypt PGP messages online?
faq.a1: Yes, when using KeychainPGP's web app. All encryption and decryption happens locally in your browser using WebAssembly compiled from Rust. No data is ever sent to a server.
faq.q2: What is PGP encryption used for?
faq.a2: PGP encryption is used to protect private messages, emails, files, and digital signatures. It uses public-key cryptography — you encrypt with someone's public key, and only their private key can decrypt it.
faq.a3: PGP generates a random session key, encrypts your message with a fast symmetric cipher, then encrypts the session key with the recipient's public key. The recipient uses their private key to decrypt the session key, then decrypts the message.
faq.q4: What is the difference between PGP and GPG?
faq.a4: PGP is the original encryption standard by Phil Zimmermann (1991). GPG (GnuPG) is a free, open-source implementation. OpenPGP is the IETF standard (RFC 4880). KeychainPGP implements OpenPGP using the Sequoia-PGP library in Rust.
faq.q5: Can I use PGP without installing software?
faq.a5: Yes. KeychainPGP offers a web-based PGP tool that runs entirely in your browser. You can generate keys, encrypt and decrypt messages without installing anything.
faq.q6: What encryption algorithm does KeychainPGP use?
faq.a6: KeychainPGP uses Ed25519 for signatures and X25519 for key agreement — modern elliptic curve algorithms providing equivalent security to RSA-3072 at a fraction of the key size, powered by Sequoia-PGP.
faq.q7: Is PGP encryption still secure in 2026?
faq.a7: Yes. PGP with modern algorithms like Ed25519/X25519 remains one of the strongest encryption standards. Its security depends on proper key management and up-to-date implementations.
faq.q8: How do I share my PGP public key?
faq.a8: Export your public key in ASCII-armored format and send it via email, chat, or publish it on your website. In KeychainPGP, go to the Keys tab and use the export function.
faq.a9: A workflow where you copy text, encrypt or decrypt with a hotkey, and paste the result. Works with any app. KeychainPGP uses Ctrl+Shift+E to encrypt and Ctrl+Shift+D to decrypt.
faq.q10: Is KeychainPGP really free and open source?
faq.a10: Yes. 100% free and open source under MIT/Apache-2.0 license. No paid tiers, no accounts, no telemetry. Funded by voluntary cryptocurrency donations.
